programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. Learn parallel stopping, backward edges and turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 4 or able to demonstrate crossovers, backward stopping, one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational

In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. Learn parallel stopping, backward edges and turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 4 or able to demonstrate crossovers, backward stopping, one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. This activity is for all women. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Hand Building: Teapots
programs.description: Create a one-of-a-kind,  hand-built teapot this summer! In this five-week workshop, you’ll learn how to  craft every element – from the body and spout to the lid and handles – using  slab-building techniques. Along the way, you’ll have the chance to get creative  with sculptural details and explore a variety of decorative finishes to bring  your vision to life. Perfect for all levels. Please note that students must supply their own pottery tools.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: House of Hockey Ball Hockey
programs.description: House of Hockey is a partnership program with the Maple Leafs Sports & Entertainment foundation that aims to give participants access to competitive sport and team building opportunities. House Leagues are community-orientated sports programs that are structured to provide participants with skill development opportunities and competition at a recreational level.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1

Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. This activity is for all women. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Hand Building: Teapots
programs.description: Create a one-of-a-kind,  hand-built teapot this summer! In this five-week workshop, you’ll learn how to  craft every element – from the body and spout to the lid and handles – using  slab-building techniques. Along the way, you’ll have the chance to get creative  with sculptural details and explore a variety of decorative finishes to bring  your vision to life. Perfect for all levels. Please note that students must supply their own pottery tools.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: House of Hockey Ball Hockey
programs.description: House of Hockey is a partnership program with the Maple Leafs Sports & Entertainment foundation that aims to give participants access to competitive sport and team building opportunities. House Leagues are community-orientated sports programs that are structured to provide participants with skill development opportunities and competition at a recreational level.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. Learn parallel stopping, backward edges and turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 4 or able to demonstrate crossovers, backward stopping, one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. This activity is for all women. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Hand Building: Teapots
programs.description: Create a one-of-a-kind,  hand-built teapot this summer! In this five-week workshop, you’ll learn how to  craft every element – from the body and spout to the lid and handles – using  slab-building techniques. Along the way, you’ll have the chance to get creative  with sculptural details and explore a variety of decorative finishes to bring  your vision to life. Perfect for all levels. Please note that students must supply their own pottery tools.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: House of Hockey Ball Hockey
programs.description: House of Hockey is a partnership program with the Maple Leafs Sports & Entertainment foundation that aims to give participants access to competitive sport and team building opportunities. House Leagues are community-orientated sports programs that are structured to provide participants with skill development opportunities and competition at a recreational level.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
